message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): IPv6 literal URL (bracketed authority) roundtrip (tick 344)

Added `distributed_http_client_handles_ipv6_literal_url` — pins that
`DistributedHttpClient::get` correctly handles URLs with bracketed
IPv6 literals like `http://[::1]:<port>/path`.

Per RFC 3986 §3.2.2, IPv6 authorities in URLs use square brackets to
disambiguate the colon in the IPv6 address from the colon separating
host:port. Per RFC 7230 §5.4, the `Host:` header preserves the
bracketed form.

Two regression modes this test catches:
1. Client strips brackets before TCP connect → `[::1]:port` and
   `::1:port` parse differently, so stripping would cause connect
   failures (or worse, connect to the wrong interpretation of the
   address).
2. Client fails to include brackets in the Host header →
   `Host: [::1]:port` is RFC-compliant and many reverse proxies key
   their routing on the bracketed form.

Test binds a loopback IPv6 listener, makes a GET request to
`http://[::1]:<port>/ipv6`, and asserts:
- happy-path response (200 + "ok") round-trips, AND
- server-received Host header contains `host: [::1]:<port>`
  (case-insensitive per RFC 7230 §3.2)

Gracefully skips with an eprintln note on CI hosts where IPv6
loopback is unavailable — the contract is only meaningful when v6 is
available. Most macOS/Linux dev + CI environments have v6 loopback.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 16s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
